Paths/Frontend Development/React Basics

React Basics

Build component-based UIs with the most popular frontend library.

React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces using reusable components.

Key Concepts

  • Components: Functions that return JSX (HTML-like syntax in JavaScript).
  • Props: Pass data from parent to child components.
  • State: Manage dynamic data within a component using `useState`.
  • Effects: Handle side effects like API calls with `useEffect`.
  • Conditional Rendering: Show different UI based on state or props.

Example

import { useState } from 'react';

function Counter() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  return (
    <div>
      <p>Count: {count}</p>
      <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>
        Increment
      </button>
    </div>
  );
}
